serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 1 piece of chicken with sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Ayam Tempra is approximately 150g, equivalent to one or two pieces of chicken with sauce, as commonly served in a Singaporean or Malaysian household meal.

nutrient_source.title: Ayam Tempra — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ayam Tempra (CR ID: F11020007). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Dishes and Composite Meals — Analogous Estimate Based on Low-GI Chicken Dish with Sweet Soy Sauce
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Ayam Tempra exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 54 (low) is estimated by analogy to cooked chicken dishes with sweet soy sauce-based marinades, which are low-carbohydrate protein-dominant preparations. The glycemic load per 150g serving is calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 3.63) / 100 ≈ 1.97. The input record cited GI 54 from an embedded GI database with confidence 0.409; this is retained as a low-GI analogous estimate pending direct measurement.

hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 348 mg/100g is borderline elevated; individuals with hypertension should request reduced kecap manis and soy sauce during preparation, and limit portion size to keep sodium intake within the 2,000 mg/day target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.42 g/100g) and high protein (16.31 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low carbohydrate content and high protein level support stable postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should pair this dish with low-GI carbohydrates and request reduced kecap manis to minimise added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.5 g/100g) and sodium (348 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat is below the 5 g/100g threshold and total fat is moderate; however, the sodium content warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease, who should request reduced soy sauce and limit portion size to manage cumulative sodium and saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spice blend; nightshade ingredients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Garlic, shallots, and tamarind contribute anti-inflammatory polyphenols; however, chilli and other nightshade ingredients may aggravate symptoms in nightshade-sensitive individuals, who should request chilli-free preparation and reduced soy sauce to limit sodium-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayam Tempra is a traditional Malay and Singaporean chicken dish in which bone-in or boneless chicken pieces are braised or stir-fried in a sauce made from kecap manis (sweet soy sauce), lime juice, shallots, garlic, chilli, and tamarind. The dish is characterised by a glossy, dark-brown sauce with a sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy flavour profile, and tender, well-seasoned chicken with a slightly caramelised exterior. It is a staple of Malay home cooking and is commonly served as part of a rice-based meal in Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, Ayam Tempra is a high-protein, moderate-fat, very low-carbohydrate dish: per 100g it provides approximately 195 kcal, 16.3g protein, 13.3g fat (of which approximately 3.5g is saturated), and only 2.4g carbohydrate. Sodium is 348 mg/100g, reflecting the soy sauce content. Dietary fibre is 1.87 g/100g, contributed by the spice and vegetable components. The dish is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate and high protein content, which supports stable blood glucose. Sodium is borderline elevated, making portion control and reduced-sauce preparation advisable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is halal and dairy-free. It contains soy as an allergen from the kecap manis. The spice blend provides antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, though nightshade-sensitive individuals should request chilli-free preparation.

GI of 54 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework; no direct GI measurement for Ayam Tempra has been published. 4.
